title: wedelia jacquinii subsp. involucrata
intro: Wedelia jacquinii subsp. involucrata is a flowering plant species that belongs to the daisy family and is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to be beneficial in treating various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The medicinal properties of Wedelia jacquinii subsp. involucrata have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and antioxidant effects. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, are used to treat various health conditions, and it is also used in traditional medicine to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
